title: gh#79: Assert handler-level + central log both fire on PATCH 500 (prevent silent regression)
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/79.
Backlog section: Tests.

## Context

PR #77 introduces two structured log lines per profile-PATCH unhandled failure, correlated by `correlationId`:

- **Handler-level** (in `routes/profiles.ts`): `{err, profileId, userId, correlationId}` with message `Profile update failed`. Carries domain context the central handler doesn't have.
- **Central error handler** (in `middleware/error-handler.ts`): `{err, method, route, correlationId}` with message `Unhandled error`. Carries route context the handler doesn't have.

Tests in #77 assert each log line independently in different test files. Nothing asserts both fire on the same PATCH failure with the same correlationId. If a future refactor accidentally drops the handler-level log (e.g., someone removes the `logger.error(...)` line in profiles.ts thinking the central handler covers it), the API tests would still pass — the regression slips through.

## Proposed change

Add one assertion to the existing PATCH 500 test in `packages/api/src/__tests__/routes/profiles.test.ts` (the test added in #77: *"returns 500 with stable code and correlation id for unexpected update failures (gh#54)"*). Specifically, spy on `logger.error` and confirm at least one call carries the `Profile update failed` message and the same correlationId as the response.

OR: add a small standalone test asserting both messages fire in the same request flow.

Either path is fine — pick whichever fits the existing test file style.

## Out of scope

- Changing the two-log-line design itself (intentional; tracks two distinct concerns).
- Equivalent coverage on the DELETE path — DELETE was hardened in `cbde4a0` without this assertion either; either backfill there in this issue or in a follow-up, editorial call.

## Acceptance

- `pnpm --filter @sms/api test` passes.
- The new assertion would fail if the `logger.error(...)` call inside the PATCH catch block in `routes/profiles.ts` is deleted.

## References

- Surfaced by the code review on [PR #77](https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/pull/77) (suggestion 3).
